title: Mid-Market AI Business Partner - Spanish speaking
description: We are seeking an intellectually curious Mid\-Market AI Business Partner (Spanish speaking) to work directly with Mid\-Market customers. This role is focused on engaging senior business leaders (often FTSE 250 \& Fortune 500 companies) in **high\-value, consultative conversations around AI transformation, data strategy, and business value creation** . This is not a traditional SDR or sales development role. Instead, it is a **strategic discovery and advisory position** , responsible for shaping early\-stage executive dialogue, influencing account strategy, and identifying where AI can unlock meaningful business outcomes. The role focuses on quality over volume, driving well‑researched consultative conversations, identifying the problems end customers are trying to resolve with AI and the use cases \& value the business are hoping to unlock. You will own the early to mid\-sales cycle; from targeted prospecting, account mapping, deep account research through to qualification and warm handoffs. Acting as the first impression and the face of the brand to executive buyers (up to c\-level), you will demonstrate strong executive presence, clear storytelling, and a deep understanding of AI and SaaS solutions to help identify opportunities for the end customer. Success in this role comes from a curious mindset to perform deep discovery, technical competency, \& a passion in AI and its application within a business. **Key Responsibilities** - Prospect and qualify new business opportunities through thoughtful, targeted outreach. - Lead executive level discovery conversations (c\-suite/c\-1\). Engage C‑suite and senior stakeholders with clear, confident, and consultative communication. - Shape account strategy, not just pipeline. This includes mapping complex organisations and stakeholders - Act as a thought partner to mid\-market customers through driving deep, structured discovery to uncover root business problems, challenge assumptions and connect technical capability with business value. - Partner closely with Account Directors on account planning \& development, research, and meeting preparation. Identify opportunities and build strategic hypotheses on where AI can deliver value - Own qualification and handoff, ensuring Account Directors receive well‑defined, high‑quality opportunities. - Maintain accurate CRM data, forecasting, and activity records. - Continuously build AI \& market expertise. Stay current on AI, SaaS, and market trends to communicate complex concepts clearly. **Qualifications** - Native or near‑native English \& Spanish with exceptional communication skills. - Minimum 1\-2 years' B2B sales experience required - 3\+ years’ experience in tech sales, inside sales, or business development (AI/SaaS/enterprise preferred). - A strong background/experience with Account Planning and Development - Strong technical competency, curiosity and ability to simplify complex topics. Background in AI, data, SaaS or digital transformation preferred - Exceptional storytelling \& communication skills - Curiosity \& problem solving to take structured approaches to discovery \& hypothesis building - Professional presence to face into executive levels - Detail‑oriented, research‑driven, and experienced with Salesforce or similar CRM tools. **Why Join** You’ll join a high‑bar sales team that values precision, credibility, and strategic impact. This role suits sales professionals who take pride in how they show up, operate with integrity, and bring discipline and insight to every executive conversation.

title: Middle Office Analyst/ Associate
description: PIMCO is a global leader in active fixed income with deep expertise across public and private markets. We invest our clients’ capital across a range of fixed income and credit opportunities, leveraging our decades of experience navigating complex debt markets. Our flexible capital base and deep relationships with issuers have helped us become one of the world’s largest providers of traditional and nontraditional solutions for companies that need financing and investors who seek strong risk\-adjusted returns. Since 1971, our people have shaped our organization through a high\-performance inclusive culture, in which we celebrate diverse thinking. We invest in our people and strive to imprint our CORE values of Collaboration, Openness, Responsibility and Excellence. We believe each of us is here to help others succeed and this has led to PIMCO being recognized as an innovator, industry thought leader and trusted advisor to our clients. **Position Description** The candidate will be working in the Middle Office team in Dublin processing OTC and non\-OTC trades encompassing Bond trades, FX, Derivatives Repos and Swaps with the aim being to provide support to the Portfolio Managers for any product traded. The primary responsibilities include regular contact with brokers to confirm trades, acting as escalation contact for settlement queries and monitoring the inhouse STP trade capture system. Thus, strong trade flow management ability and good settlement experience are vital. Furthermore, the Associate will be involved in interaction with the Dublin and the Newport Beach offices. * Daily interaction with Portfolio Managers, Compliance, and Investment Operations. * Monitor proprietary trade capture system: ensure accurate trade capture and completeness of tickets. * Complete initial confirmation with the broker for trades executed in London office * Trade floor support: run various daily trade\-flow reports and any ad hoc reports requested by Portfolio Management team. * Interaction with Back Office support functions: escalation contact for trade settlement. The back\-office support is outsourced to State Street, daily contact through telephone and email. There is also direct contact with Brokers to resolve issues and support State Street as point of escalation. * The team work on a weekly alternating shift pattern of 8\.00am – 5\.00pm, 9\.30am – 6\.30pm and 11\.30am until close. On average the late shift is shorter, but a willingness to work later hours as required is essential for the role. **Position Requirements** * Proven experience in a similar role within Asset Management or Investment Banking. * Excellent attention to detail. Strong analytical ability is essential * Strong verbal and written communication skills are vital to work within the firm’s demanding and focused environment. * Capability to manage multiple tasks with tight time deadlines and possess strong problem\-solving ability. * Flexibility and ability to work under pressure with a fair degree of autonomy as well as in a team environment is essential. PIMCO works on a flat structure so ability to work with all levels of firm. * Good working knowledge of MS Word, Excel, Access, MS Outlook and Bloomberg. Basic knowledge of other MS programs such as PowerPoint preferred. SQL and programming skills are a plus. * Educated to degree level or equivalent is an advantage * Clear commitment to PIMCO’s culture, values and approach Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Statement *PIMCO recruits and hires qualified candidates without regard to race, national origin, ancestry, religion (including religious dress and grooming practices), sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender (including gender identity and expression), age, military or veteran status, disability (physical or mental), any factor prohibited by law, and as such affirms in policy and practice to support and promote the concept of equal employment opportunity and affirmative action, in accordance with all applicable federal, state, provincial and municipal laws. The company also prohibits discrimination on other basis such as medical condition, or marital status under applicable laws.* Applicants with Disabilities *PIMCO is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. We provide reasonable accommodation for qualified individuals with disabilities, including veterans, in job application procedures. If you have any difficulty using our online system due to a disability and you would like to request an accommodation, you may contact us at 949\-720\-7744 and leave a message. This is a dedicated line designed exclusively to assist job seekers with disabilities to apply online. Only messages left for this purpose will be considered. A response to your request may take up to two business days.*

title: Network Operations Engineer, GEN Operations
description: ### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in a technical field, or equivalent practical experience. * 3 years of experience with MPLS VPN, Firewalls, Data center Networking, and Virtual Extensible LAN (VXLAN). * Experience with core networking protocols and technologies, including TCP/IP, routing protocols (BGP, OSPF, ISIS), and LAN switching. * Experience coding in Python or Go to develop automation solutions for network monitoring and management. ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Certifications: Professional or expert\-level industry certifications such as CCNP, CCIE, JNCIE, or Google's Professional Cloud Network Engineer certification. * Experience performing network troubleshooting and participating in global on\-call rotations to maintain service level objectives (SLOs). * Familiarity with Data Center Architecture, Wide Area Network (WAN) design, and Wireless Communications. * A solid understanding of Linux/Unix fundamentals, including system resource monitoring and shell scripting. * Demonstrated ability to manage projects with some complexity, contribute to technical documentation, and mentor junior team members. **About the job** ----------------- Google is proud to boast a network that provides service to millions of Internet users around the world. The Network Engineering team is responsible for operating that network reliably and at scale. As a member of the team, you have a direct impact on design and feature enhancements to keep our systems running smoothly. You also ensure that network operations are safe and efficient by monitoring network performance, coordinating planned maintenance, adjusting hardware components and responding to network connectivity issues. Google's complex network generates a constant stream of challenges which require you to continually be innovative with an evolving set of technologies. Keeping the network reliable ensures that our users stay connected with our suite of applications, products and services. In this role, you will operate Google Enterprise Network within Service Level Objective (SLO) to provide a reliable, secure and consistent networking experience to Googlers through effective monitoring, faster service restoration, efficient tools and processes.Behind everything our users see online is the architecture built by the Technical Infrastructure team to keep it running. From developing and maintaining our data centers to building the next\-generation of Google platforms, we make Google's product portfolio possible. We're proud to be our engineers' engineers and love voiding warranties by taking things apart so we can rebuild them. We keep our networks up and running, ensuring our users have the best and fastest experience possible.Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €100000 \- €103000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits Learn more about benefits at Google.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * Serve as a subject matter expert in multiple network domains (e.g., Routing, Switching, Wireless, Firewalls, SD\-WAN, Network Automation). * Manage the network escalations and provide technical leadership during critical service\-impacting events, working closely with the Network Operations Center (NOC). * Lead initiatives to meet and exceed Service Level Objectives (SLOs), aiming for a 99\.95% network availability target globally. * Oversee the operational aspects of New Product Introductions (NPIs), including designs like NextGEN WAN and Office, involving testing, defining operational requirements, developing support models, and creating documentation. * Develop scripts and tools (often using Python or Go) to automate network diagnostics, configuration, and monitoring tasks. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

title: Network Support Engineer
description: **Network Support Engineer — Juniper \& Aruba specialist at a leading European technology firm** **€55,000 \+ €5,000 On\-Call Allowance \| Dublin \| Office Based** Our client is a pan\-European technology business with around 1,500 engineers operating across 10 countries, delivering mission\-critical network and security infrastructure for some of Europe's largest organisations. Their Dublin NOC is the hub for supporting a high\-profile customer base across Juniper and Aruba Clearpass environments. This is a 1st and 2nd line network support role with genuine progression built in. You'll start on the NOC, supporting customer environments remotely and occasionally on\-site, while working alongside senior architects on staging and project work. The intention is to develop this person into project engineering — it's not just a support job, it's a launchpad. **WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING** • First and second line support for Juniper and Aruba Clearpass customer environments • Incident management from initial triage through to resolution — with full SLA accountability • Daily health checks across customer networks, producing technical reports for review • Escalation to senior engineers and vendor TAC teams where required • Patch management and preventative maintenance of managed infrastructure • Occasional site visits to customer locations across Ireland • Hands\-on involvement with senior engineers on project staging as you develop **TECH STACK** • Juniper Networks • HPE Aruba Clearpass • Aruba Wireless • JNCIA / JNCIP • NOC / NSOC tooling \& RMM platforms **WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR** • 3–4 years of network support experience — NOC or NSOC background ideal • Juniper certification essential — JNCIA minimum, JNCIP preferred; Juniper certs carry the most weight here • HPE Aruba Clearpass experience required — certification a strong plus • Strong troubleshooting instincts — you diagnose methodically and don't guess • Excellent written and verbal English — customer\-facing from day one • Full Irish or European driving licence required for occasional site visits • Comfortable in an office\-based environment — this role is 5 days a week in Dublin **THE PACKAGE** • €55,000 base salary • €5,000 on\-call allowance (optional rota) • On\-call not required for the first 6 months • 5 days per week in the Dublin office **WHY THIS ROLE STANDS OUT** • Pan\-European business with scale, stability, and serious technical credibility • Work alongside some of Ireland's leading Aruba Clearpass and Juniper architects — you'll learn fast • Genuine development path into project engineering — this isn't just talk, it's the plan • Formal training and certification supported and encouraged • On\-call is optional and not expected for the first 6 months — a respectful onboarding approach • Mission\-critical customer base — finance, healthcare, public sector — serious work that sharpens you **Juniper certified, Dublin\-based, and ready to level up? Let's talk.** **Apply below or reach out directly — all applications handled in confidence.**

description: **Overview** Joining the Client team as a Sales Operations Analyst supporting the Options Sales business, you will be responsible for Susquehanna’s options sales desk’s post trade processes to provide a seamless client experience. This role involves working closely with our sales traders, traders, and counterparties. Due to the high volume of trades, we are looking for candidates who have exceptional attention to detail. This role will offer a great exposure to a front office Sales \& Trading environment and will be an excellent opportunity if you have strong communication skills and an interest in financial markets. Being directly exposed to client interactions, a good level of commercial awareness is desired. **In This Role You Will** * Responsible for post trade processes * Execution and allocation of orders through internal/external systems * Act as a first point of contact for any issues related to crossing and settling trades across multiple exchanges, countries and clients * Address each client’s specific post trade requirements and tailor the process to their needs. For example, clients trading on CFD or making sure trades with FTT are accounted for correctly. * Identify opportunities to improve the overall process, automate tools where possible in order to enhance the broader desk’s performance and efficiency. This could include working with the static data team to improve our flex trading process and working with app support and developers to improve the desks tools * Liaise with other teams to develop and work on ad hoc projects aimed at improving the team’s workflow **What We're Looking For** * Minimum of a Bachelors’ degree * Ability to prioritise and complete multiple tasks in a fast paced, dynamic environment * High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. Python a plus * Strong communicator (verbal and written) * Ability to work under pressure and multi\-task * Ambitious and self\-motivated, not afraid to ask questions and use own initiative * Effective problem solving and analytical skills; including effective troubleshooting in a fast\-paced, time\-critical environment * Experience in financial markets is not required although we welcome those with experience * Excellent attention to detail **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. title: Patent Paralegal
description: Join the leader in entertainment innovation and help us design the future. At Dolby, science meets art, and high tech means more than computer code. As a member of the Dolby team, you’ll see and hear the results of your work everywhere, from movie theaters to smartphones. We continue to revolutionize how people create, deliver, and enjoy entertainment worldwide. To do that, we need the absolute best talent. We’re big enough to give you all the resources you need, and small enough so you can make a real difference and earn recognition for your work. We offer a collegial culture, challenging projects, and excellent compensation and benefits, not to mention a Flex Work approach that is truly flexible to support where, when, and how you do your best work. We are a well\-known, prestigious international technology company with a global hub office in Dublin, Ireland. We are looking for an experienced Patent Paralegal to join our European patent group and protect rights to our innovations. This job is a hybrid role, requiring 2 days a week (usually Tuesdays and Wednesdays) in the office. **In this role as a Patent Paralegal, you will:** * Review patent correspondence and extract relevant information * Initiate filing of EP and PCT applications, including preparing complex legal documents for such patent filings \& ensuring legal requirements are met for filings * Deal with formal requirements such as preparing and filing of subsequent documents with the European Patent Office (EPO) * Maintain records related to the prosecution of patent applications, including tracking case status, preparing responses, \& managing communications ensuring accuracy * Support special projects related to our worldwide patent portfolio * Work collaboratively and positively providing comprehensive and professional paralegal support with internal patent attorneys, patent agents, and international law firms utilizing in\-depth knowledge of foreign patent rules and requirements * Actively establish strong working relationships, freely share information, and collaborate with IP and Legal department personnel in Ireland and worldwide * Update policies and procedures to accommodate changes in internal accounts and legal considerations * Ensure compliance with patent handling procedures * Provide other support services such as preparing reports and presentations, and providing backup to other Patent Paralegals and docketing personnel * Demonstrate flexibility and teamwork through willingness to attend evening meetings 1 day a week to coordinate with teammates across time zones * Benefit from the company’s commitment to supporting their employees with professional development **Qualifications:** * 2\+ years in a similar role * CIPA IP Paralegal qualification is highly desirable. Previous experience as a Patent Paralegal or a background in Intellectual Property patent prosecution would be an advantage * Extensive experience with and knowledge of EPO applications, such as EPO Online Account, CMS, EPO filing software, EPO Mailbox * Experience with Anaqua or other docketing systems is a plus * Patent research skills are desirable, including working knowledge of online research tools in PAIR, Espacenet, PATENTSCOPE, and other foreign patent office databases * The ability and willingness to learn new software applications * Excellent work ethic, attention to detail, and intellectual curiosity * Strong desire and flexibility to work with an international team in other time zones * Adaptable, open\-minded, and efficiency\-oriented * Extremely organised, eye for detail and ability to efficiently manage workload \& prioritise * Collaborative, with willingness to share information and facilitate others’ success, team\-oriented mindset * Confidence to diplomatically share contrasting opinions and ability to productively disagree with colleagues to find the best solution to improve processes Dolby Hiring Entity: 77 Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Block C Grand Canal Docklands Dublin D02 VK60 Ireland

title: Personal Lines Administrator
description: **Introduction** At Gallagher, we help clients face risk with confidence because we believe that when businesses are protected, they’re free to grow, lead, and innovate. You’ll be backed by our digital ecosystem: a client\-centric suite of consulting tools making it easier for you to meet your clients where they want to be met. Advanced data and analytics providing a comprehensive overview of the risk landscape is at your fingertips. Here, you’re not just improving clients' risk profiles, you’re building trust. We value curiosity, celebrate new ideas, and empower you to take ownership of your career while making a meaningful impact for the businesses we serve. If you’re ready to bring your unique perspective to a place where your work truly matters; think of Gallagher. **Overview** Gallagher Ireland, part of the globally renowned Arthur J. Gallagher Group, is seeking a dedicated and detail\-oriented Personal Lines Administrator to join our team. This is a full\-time, permanent position based in Dublin. With over 100 years of combined insurance experience, Gallagher Ireland offers tailored business, personal, and financial insurance solutions to protect what matters most to our customers. As part of a global organisation with over 56,000 employees and a presence in more than 130 countries, you’ll be joining a team that values innovation, service excellence, and a commitment to professional growth. **How You'll Make An Impact** In this role, you’ll play a vital part in supporting our Personal Lines team by ensuring the smooth administration of insurance policies and providing exceptional service to our clients. Your responsibilities will include: * Providing general administrative support to the Personal Lines team. * Assisting in the preparation, issuance, and processing of personal lines insurance policies, endorsements, and renewals. * Maintaining and updating client records in the company database, ensuring accuracy and confidentiality. * Responding to client inquiries via phone, email, or in person, providing information on policy details, claims, and billing. * Coordinating with providers to obtain quotes, process endorsements, and resolve discrepancies. * Supporting the team in preparing reports, presentations, and documentation for client meetings. * Monitoring and following up on outstanding client requirements to ensure timely task completion. * Assisting in the claims process by gathering necessary documentation and liaising with relevant contacts. * Ensuring compliance with company policies, industry regulations, and legal requirements. * Participating in team meetings and contributing to process improvement initiatives. **About You** Here’s what you’ll bring to the role: * Proven experience in a busy office administration role, with excellent communication and organisational skills. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (essential). * Experience using Applied software (advantageous). * Strong time management skills and the ability to multi\-task in a fast\-paced, customer\-focused environment. * A self\-motivated, enthusiastic, and team\-oriented approach with a keen eye for detail.

The successful candidate will play a key role in building scalable cloud platforms, implementing Infrastructure as Code, and supporting secure software delivery pipelines. Working closely with engineering, security, and operations teams, you will help deliver well\-architected solutions that align with industry best practices and regulatory requirements. This position is ideal for someone who enjoys solving complex infrastructure challenges and contributing to the evolution of cloud\-native platforms. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, build, and maintain secure, scalable, and resilient AWS cloud infrastructure * Implement and manage Infrastructure as Code (IaC) solutions using Terraform * Develop and maintain automation frameworks for cloud provisioning, deployment, and management * Support cloud networking, security, and governance initiatives across AWS environments * Design and manage multi\-account AWS architectures, landing zones, and cloud governance frameworks * Collaborate with engineering teams to build and optimise CI/CD pipelines and GitOps workflows * Implement security best practices including IAM, encryption, key management, and access controls * Monitor platform health, performance, reliability, and availability across cloud environments * Support incident management, troubleshooting, and root\-cause analysis activities * Contribute to cloud architecture decisions and platform strategy initiatives * Drive continuous improvement across automation, security, scalability, and operational processes * Work closely with security and compliance teams to ensure adherence to regulatory standards **Key Requirements** **Essential Skills \& Experience** * 5\+ years of experience in Platform Engineering, Cloud Engineering, Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), or Software Engineering * Minimum 3 years of hands\-on AWS experience * Proven experience designing and supporting production\-grade cloud infrastructure * Strong understanding of AWS networking, security, identity management, and governance * Advanced networking knowledge including firewalls, DNS, load balancing, proxies, and network scripting or programming language such as Python, Go, or Shell * Experience implementing cloud security best practices, including IAM, encryption, and KMS * Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across technical teams **Desirable Skills \& Experience** * Experience within financial services, fintech, insurance, or other regulated industries * Experience with multi\-account AWS environments, Control Tower, and landing zone implementations * Knowledge of Zero Trust security architectures * Hands\-on experience with Docker and Kubernetes/EKS * Experience with monitoring and observability tools such as Grafana, Prometheus, Splunk, Dynatrace, or AWS\-native services * Experience optimising cloud cost, performance, and operational efficiency * Exposure to cloud architecture and platform strategy initiatives * Participation in cloud engineering communities or open\-source projects

Responsibilities:** * Design, implement, and maintain cloud infrastructure in AWS using Terraform, including development of reusable modules. * Operate and enhance production Kubernetes clusters using Helm, Karpenter, ArgoCD, and AWS EKS. * Implement and maintain GitOps\-driven CI/CD pipelines for infrastructure and application deployments. * Improve platform reliability through proactive monitoring, alerting, and observability using Prometheus, Grafana, CheckMK, and internal tools. * Automate operational processes using Bash, Python, Go, and Rust. * Troubleshoot production incidents across infrastructure, networking, containers, and application layers. * Support and optimise large\-scale, database\-driven systems (MySQL required; PostgreSQL a plus). * Contribute to capacity planning, scaling strategies, and performance optimisation initiatives. * Drive operational excellence improvements and incident reduction initiatives. * Manage and optimise centralised logging using Elasticsearch, Filebeat, rsyslog, and Kibana. * Maintain documentation and participate in a 24x7 on\-call rotation. * Collaborate with development teams on tools development, service improvements, and change management activities. **About You:** We’re looking for someone who not only has the right skills but also embodies our guiding principles: * **The Owner** — You take accountability, act with integrity, and treat challenges as opportunities. * **The Ambitious Maverick** — You bring bold ideas, challenge the status quo, and are unafraid to take smart risks. * **The Radical Optimist** — You voices to thrive. **Skills \& Experience:** **Essential:** * 3\+ years of experience in technical support, systems administration, or platform/infrastructure engineering. * Proficiency in LAMP/LNMP environments. * Hands\-on experience with Kubernetes and Docker. * Experience with modern CI/CD tooling (GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, or equivalent). * Strong Linux systems knowledge and troubleshooting capability. * Solid understanding of networking fundamentals (DNS, TCP/IP, HTTP, load balancing, TLS, caching). * Experience with cloud automation on AWS and Terraform. * Experience with Elasticsearch and log management. * Scripting skills in Python, Bash, or similar languages. * Experience administering MySQL databases. * Excellent troubleshooting and analytical skills. **Desirable:** * Experience with configuration management tools such as Chef/Cinc. * PostgreSQL database experience. * Experience leveraging AI tools to improve code quality and establishing AI\-related workflows. **What we offer:** 🏡 Hybrid work: 2–3 remote days \+ 20 days abroad per year ✅ Essentials: health cover, pension, taxsaver benefit, extra leave 🎁 Flexible benefits: personalize with health, wellbeing \& learning options 🌈 D\&I commitment: parental leave, Inclusive Day, gender equity in rewards L\&D Support: Study Assistance and Study Leave 🎉 Unbox Your World: Take 1 day leave per year as short notice title: Production Engineer (Kubernetes)
description: Crusoe is on a mission *to accelerate the abundance of energy and intelligence* . As the only vertically integrated AI infrastructure company built from the ground up, we own and operate each layer of the stack — from electrons to tokens — to power the world's most ambitious AI workloads. When you join Crusoe, you join a team that is building the future, faster. We're in the midst of the greatest industrial revolution of our time. The demand for AI compute is boundless, and power is a bottleneck. We're solving that — with an energy\-first approach that makes AI infrastructure better for the world and faster for the people innovating with AI. We're looking for problem\-solving, opportunity\-finding teammates with a sense of urgency, who believe in the scale of our ambition and thrive on a path not fully paved — people who want to grow their careers alongside a team of experts across energy, manufacturing, data center construction, and cloud services. If you want to do the most meaningful work of your career, help our customers and partners advance their AI strategies, and be part of a high\-performing team that believes in each other, come build with us at Crusoe. **About This Role:** At Crusoe, our Production Engineering team plays a pivotal role in ensuring the reliability and performance of our infrastructure. Production Engineer at Crusoe is dedicated to detecting, analyzing, and preventing issues to maintain high Service Level Agreements (SLAs) through Service Level Indicators (SLIs) and Service Level Objectives (SLOs). Through automation and proactive remediation, our Production Engineers resolve common errors automatically, then advise various engineering teams how to build resilient code. We anticipate and resolve issues before they impact our customers, conduct thorough post\-mortems, and drive continuous improvement. Our customer\-centric approach ensures that clients always have access to the virtual machines they depend on. This role is crucial for maintaining the "gold standard" reliability and performance of Crusoe's AI platform. The ideal candidate has experience in Production Engineering practices, understanding of distributed systems, networking, Linux, and a passion for automation and problem\-solving. This is a full\-time position. **What You’ll Be Working On:** * Building Kubernetes Platform: Focus on scaling tooling and features dedicated to Crusoe's Managed Kubernetes and Managed VM platforms for external customers. * Collaboration and Planning: Collaborate with the team in morning stand\-up meetings to discuss ongoing projects, recent incidents, and priorities for the day. Collaborate on action plans for deploying new data centers or retrofitting existing ones. Work closely with software engineers, advising on best practices for resilient code and reviewing changes before deployment. * System Monitoring and Alerting: Review overnight alerts and system performance metrics to ensure everything is running smoothly. Analyze system logs and develop tools to enhance our monitoring capabilities. * Incident Response and Problem Solving: Engage in incident response drills, post\-mortems, and root cause analysis sessions to learn from past issues and prevent future ones. Resolve common errors automatically through automation and proactive remediation. * Performance Monitoring and Optimization: Stay focused on maintaining high SLIs and SLOs, ensuring that our infrastructure remains robust and reliable for our customers. * Documentation and Knowledge Sharing: Document work, share insights with the team, and plan for the next day's challenges, always with a customer\-centric mindset. **What You’ll Bring to the Team:** * Production Engineering Experience: 3\-6 years of professional Production Engineer experience. * Kubernetes: Experience building Kubernetes platforms or Kubernetes controllers * Server Hardware and Provisioning: Exposure to server\-class hardware \& provisioning. * Distributed Systems Architecture: Understanding of distributed system architecture; exposure to common design patterns, reliability, and scaling. * Infrastructure Design: Basic understanding of infrastructure design: Familiarity with the operational trade\-offs of network, storage, and RPC serving designs. * Programming Proficiency: Proficiency with at least one programming language (Python, Go, or similar). * Observability Tooling: Exposure to Observability tooling and philosophy: logging, monitoring, and alerting tools. * Operating Systems: Experience with Unix/Linux environments. * Networking Fundamentals: Understanding of network fundamentals: Basics of TCP/IP and network programming. * Information Security Awareness: Awareness of basic information security best practices. * Education: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, related field, or self\-educated in computer science fundamentals. * Soft Skills: Strong communication skills. * Company Values: Embody the Company values. **Benefits:** Crusoe also offers a competitive benefits package designed to support financial security, health, and overall well\-being, including pension contributions, private health and dental insurance, income protection, life assurance and more. **Compensation:** Compensation will be paid as salary or hourly. title: Project Engineer
description: **Overview** **About RoviSys:** RoviSys is an independent system integrator with year\-over\-year growth in Ireland, Europe, and world\-wide. RoviSys provides a variety of information and automation services in many industries globally. RoviSys Ireland formed in 2022, with the Dublin Office open in May 2024\. Dublin is part of RoviSys Europe group and currently operates closely with our European HQ located in Utrecht in The Nederland’s. Manufactures and systems we are currently working on in Ireland are including but not limited to Emerson DeltaV, Rockwell, Siemens PCS7 and S7, Schneider, Honeywell IQSET and Trend PLCs. SCADA include, Ignition, Aveva, AVEVA PI, IQ Vision, Cimplicity, and iFIX. **About The Opportunity** Project Engineers are responsible for leading engineering teams that design and implement customer automation projects. Project engineers take a comprehensive approach to solving problems, learning the details of each project’s mechanical, electrical, and software systems. **Responsibilities** **Software Design \& Development:** * Leading design and develop control system software for PLCs, DCS, SCADA, MES and historian platforms. * Create and maintain detailed design documentation to support system architecture and functionality. * Assists Project Manager in identifying changes in project scope and developing change orders * Serves as a technical contact for customers **Hardware Design \& Testing** * Design electrical control panels and oversee their testing to ensure compliance with specifications and safety standards. **Project Execution \& Validation** * Lead and conduct Factory Acceptance Testing to validate system performance prior to deployment. * Lead site commissioning activities for both hardware and software components. * Perform Site Acceptance Testing to confirm system functionality in the live environment. * On site commissioning A Project Engineer will typically work in a project team of 2 – 20 and will be expected to work closely with the project manager. Site location can vary, currently are in Ireland, Netherlands, Germany, UK, Spain, Nordic’s and more across the EU. As RoviSys operates a platform\-independent model, we engage with a wide variety of industrial systems. This creates a unique opportunity to work in a diverse and fast\-paced environment where continuous development and growth are part of everyday work. **Qualifications** * Bachelor of Science (Level 8\) in Engineering, Engineering Technology, Computer Science or and equivalent qualification. * 5\+ years of experience in automation * Eligible to work and travel in Ireland and Europe without sponsorship. * Full Irish Drivers License * Attend the Dublin Office on a daily basis * Strong background in industrial automation and control systems. * Hands\-on experience with PLC/DCS programming and SCADA configuration. * Familiarity with validation protocols and quality standards in regulated industries. * Experience in leading a team of engineers in a project environment * Excellent problem\-solving ability * Strong communication skills. * As a minimum, you must be proficient on one or more of the following platforms: + Rockwell ControlLogix/CompactLogix, PlantPAx, or FactoryTalk + Emerson DeltaV + Schneider Electric Modicon PLCs, EcoStruxure, PowerSCADA, Power Monitoring Expert (PME) + Siemens S7, PCS7, or WinCC + Ignition + Or similar IT, automation, or MES platform Open to travel is a must.

title: Project Technical Engineer
description: **Job Title:** Project Technical Engineer **Company:** Distag QCS **Location:** Tullow **Reporting To:** Product Line Manager **About Distag QCS:** Distag QCS is a leading provider of components and solutions to Original Equipment Manufacturers \& Distributors. We specialize in Trailer Parts, OEM Bespoke Components, Hydraulic Systems \& Power Transmission components. **Job Description**: As a Project Technical Engineer at Distag QCS, you will play a crucial role in bridging the gap between Supplier, Customer and internal Distag Sales, Purchasing and Technical Teams, developing new products \& working on various projects mainly within our OEM Bespoke Team. You will collaborate closely with our supplier base to ensure they uphold the highest standards of product quality and performance, whilst receiving requests from our Sales team on various projects to develop new products and improve existing. **Key Responsibilities:** * Cross\-functional Collaboration: Collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams, including Sales, Procurement, Engineering \& Quality assurance to ensure alignment on supplier requirements, specifications, and expectations. * Project Management: Develop and maintain project schedules, milestones, and budgets for new product development projects. Coordinate activities across cross\-functional teams, including engineering, manufacturing, marketing, and quality assurance, to ensure timely and successful project execution. * Customer Support: Provide technical support and expertise to internal teams and customers during the product development process and after product launch. Collaborate with suppliers to implement best practices and drive process optimization. * Supplier Development: Provide guidance and support to suppliers to help them improve their processes, capabilities, and performance. * Professional Development: Stay abreast of industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in new product development. **Qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in either \-Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Business, Supply Chain Management or equivalent experience. * Previous experience in a manufacturing or engineering environment is preferred. * Effective communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build strong relationships with suppliers and internal stakeholders \& customers. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and experience with ERP systems is preferred. * Willingness to travel to customers. Supplier visits in China \& India are not required but desired. **Benefits:** * Competitive salary * Comprehensive benefits package * Opportunities for professional development and growth * Collaborative and dynamic work environment * Chance to make a meaningful impact on product quality and customer satisfaction *Note: This job description is intended to convey information essential to understanding the scope of the position and is not exhaustive. title: Pump Technician (Drainage)
description: **June 2026** Established in 1997, Superdrain Limited we are one of Leinster’s leading drainage and environmental contractors. We currently have a permanent technical position available for a Pump Technician to join our large, multidisciplinary team. Entry\-level applicants may be considered based on their previous experience, technical aptitude, and willingness to learn. Previous mechanical, electrical, plumbing, or industrial maintenance experience would be a distinct advantage for consideration of a more advanced role. Please visit this web page for information before applying: https://superdrain.ie/services/pumps\-mechanical/ **The Role** The successful candidate will assist with the installation, repair, maintenance, testing, and commissioning of pumping equipment and associated assets. The work will primarily be carried out across our growing portfolio of major blue\-chip clients, SMEs, and leading facilities management companies. Key responsibilities will include: * Assisting with new pump and pumping\-system installations * Diagnosing faults and carrying out repairs to existing equipment and assets * Assisting with equipment testing and commissioning * Performing preventive maintenance and scheduled inspections * Completing work in accordance with maintenance schedules, manufacturers’ specifications, and company procedures * Maintaining accurate service, inspection, and maintenance records * Working safely and professionally within drainage, wastewater, industrial, and commercial environments * Building and maintaining effective working relationships with colleagues, clients, and site representatives * Supporting the wider technical team as required Experience in maintenance, plumbing, mechanical systems, pumps, or industrial control panels would be a distinct advantage. **Place of Employment** The role will be based primarily in Dublin. However, the successful candidate must be available to work in other areas of Ireland when required. Working Hours and On\-Call Requirements The standard working week is 40 hours, Monday to Friday. The successful candidate will be required to participate in the company’s on\-call roster. Due to the emergency nature of our work, it is a condition of employment that reasonable overtime, early starts, and out\-of\-hours work are undertaken when required. **Training and Development** The successful candidate must be willing and able to undertake a QQI Level 6 qualification in Industrial Electrical Systems within the first 12 months of employment. The cost of this qualification will be paid in full by the company. Additional job\-specific training and certification will be provided where appropriate. **Qualifications and Experience** Applicants should have: * A full, clean Category B driving licence, which is mandatory * Previous mechanical, electrical, plumbing, maintenance, or technical experience, preferably within an industrial or commercial environment * A relevant mechanical or technical qualification, which would be a distinct advantage * A valid Safe Pass, Manual Handling certificate, and Confined Space certificate, although training may be provided for the right candidate * The ability to demonstrate technical knowledge, practical ability, and a willingness to develop new skills * A strong aptitude for fault\-finding and problem\-solving * Good computer literacy * Good organisational, communication, and reporting skills * Excellent written and spoken English * Excellent customer service skills and a professional, positive attitude A Category C driving licence with a valid Driver CPC would be a distinct advantage. **Personal Attributes** The successful candidate will be: * An energetic, hands\-on self\-starter * Safety\-conscious and committed to maintaining high health and safety standards * Detail\-oriented and focused on achieving high\-quality results * Comfortable working within the drainage industry and its associated environments * Dependable, organised, and capable of working independently or as part of a team * Fully flexible regarding overtime, on\-call duties, early starts, and nationwide travel * Professional and respectful when working on client sites **Application Process** **Please note that no telephone calls will be accepted by our office in relation to this position.** Applications must be submitted **through Indeed only** and must include: * An up\-to\-date CV * A covering note outlining your relevant experience and suitability for the role Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company car * Company pension * Sick pay Licence/Certification: * B Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

LetsGetChecked's end\-to\-end model includes manufacturing, logistics, lab analysis, physician support, and prescription fulfillment. Founded in 2015 and co\-headquartered in Dublin and Atlanta, LetsGetChecked empowers people to take control of their health and live longer, happier lives. Our QA Engineers take pride in ensuring the quality of our products that drive our core mission of empowering individuals, corporations, universities, public sector, and health insurance companies to use technology in a simple but powerful way to give them greater control of their healthcare needs. This role supports our products, operations, and growth with the tools and solutions to meet market demand. You will be at the intersection of product engineering, QA automation, and complex data environments. You will be deeply experienced in at least two, passionate about all three, and conversant with stakeholders in all. You will join us in ensuring the quality of our large scale, distributed platform spanning across multiple geographic locations. Our platform resides fully in AWS, operates in dozens of markets globally and focuses on ease of use for our customers along with integrity \& security for the data they trust us with. **Position Description** * Research, develop and apply new innovative technologies to increase the quality of our products * Be an advocate of state\-of\-the\-art QA automation technology and engineering approaches * You will work as part of a close\-knit product engineering team * Drive the quality of our new products and solutions that transform our industry * Develop test frameworks and automated tests for our core services, UI’s, and API’s using the best industry standards for mission critical services * Work with autonomy to find solutions to complex problems and be flexible and responsive to changing requirements and priorities * Be a team member on a variety of projects including new product / component development, proofs of concept, and reengineering initiatives among others * The ability for critical thinking outside your immediate sphere of responsibility * Have experience with commercial products and environments (not just internally focused) * Have a collaborative working style and contribute to the development of our technical communities across the company **What We Expect From You** * BS or MS in Computer Science (or related technical discipline) or equivalent industry experience * 3\+ years hands\-on experience designing and maintaining UI (responsive web apps) and API test automation frameworks * Strong experience writing tests in an OO language (preferably C\#) * Experience integrating test tools with automated CI/CD pipelines on AWS * Expertise communicating and maintaining test results, including dashboard visualizations * Comfortable providing input on issues that impact quality, product performance and system stability/reliability * Demonstrable experience creating test cases from requirements and providing recommendations for new methods and criteria to be tested * Experience in UI Automation Frameworks (preferably Playwright or Cypress) * Strong interest or experience in leveraging AI\-powered testing tools and LLMs * Experience in RestAssured, Postman or other API testing frameworks * Experience with full lifecycle software development, the associated tools, processes and methodologies * An excellent communicator (in English), proactive and self\-sufficient, with strong organizational, time management and problem\-solving skills * Proven ability to break down and clearly define problems * Ability to establish credibility and build relationships at all levels * Passion for best quality practice and continuous professional development **Our Preferred Qualifications** * Experience using Selenium * Experience working in geo\-distributed Cloud environments (preferably AWS) * Experience testing data science and business intelligence capabilities * Experience with container technologies, e.g. Docker, Kubernetes * Experience of data management environments (e.g. SQL Server, T\-SQL, NoSQL, Hadoop, Kafka, Spark, Elastic etc) * Knowledge of Information Security concepts * US Healthcare experience **Benefits** Alongside a base salary range of €47,900 to €64,900 we offer a range of benefits including: * Health insurance and an Employee Assistance Programme * Pension * Annual Compensation Reviews * 3 paid volunteer days per year * Free monthly LetsGetChecked tests as we are not only focused on the well being of our patients but also the well being of our teams * A referral bonus programme to reward you for helping us hire the best talent * Internal Opportunities and Careers Clinics to help you progress your career within the company * Maternity, Paternity, Parental and Wedding leave * LetsGetChecked has a flexible annual leave policy **Why LetsGetChecked** At LetsGetChecked, we are revolutionizing healthcare by making it more accessible, convenient, and personalized. Our mission is to empower individuals with the knowledge and tools they need to manage their health proactively, so they can live longer, happier lives. By joining our team, you will be part of a dynamic and innovative company that is dedicated to improving lives through cutting\-edge technology and compassionate care. We value our employees and invest in their growth, offering opportunities for professional development and career advancement. Together, we can make a meaningful impact on the future of healthcare and help people take control of their health journey. Join us in our commitment to transforming healthcare for the better. **Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion** At LetsGetChecked, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment that celebrates diversity in all its forms. We believe that the diversity of thought, background, and experience strengthens our teams and drives innovation. We are an equal\-opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, color, place of birth, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, military service status, or disability status. Our goal is to ensure that everyone feels valued and empowered to thrive. Please review our Candidate Privacy Policy. To learn more about LetsGetChecked and our mission to help people live longer, healthier lives please visit https://www.letsgetchecked.com/careers/

title: QA/QC Specialist
description: **Role Summary** As part of our growing Quality team, the QA/QC Specialist will play a key part in the organisation's focus on Quality and will underpin the culture of continuous improvement. Reporting to the Head of QHSE and ESG and working closely with the Quality Engineer, the QA/QC Specialist will demonstrate outstanding attention to detail, data gathering and analysis expertise as they work to support the delivery of an exceptional customer experience. This is an excellent opportunity for a driven Quality professional who takes pride in their work and is keen to progress their career in a growing industry. **Company Profile** Capital Switchgear is a leading Low Voltage switchgear manufacturer, based in Rathcoole, Co Dublin. With over 50 years of expertise in switchgear design, manufacture, and installation, Capital prides itself on its high\-quality, reliable products and a professional personal service tailored to each project. We are committed to supporting, developing, and empowering our employees throughout their journey with us. **Key Responsibilities** *Quality Control and Assurance:* * Complete and document Quality checks throughout all stages of the manufacturing process. * Work closely with the Production team to ensure their consistent delivery of standard checks. * Process audits of the manufacturing processes to ensure that SOPs are being adhered to, and finished products comply with both internal and customer requirements. * Compile and analyse data from Quality checks to identify patterns and potential issues. * Provide regular input to the Quality team on outcomes from QA/QC checks. * Ensure any non\-conformances are recorded and acted upon promptly. * Complete non\-conformance investigations where required in line with agreed timescales, with appropriate monitoring and reporting. * Provide support for production trials, root cause analysis (RCAs) and improvement projects. * Maintain accurate records of Quality related processes and procedures. * Work closely with the Quality Engineer to draft, maintain and update technical documents related to internal Quality systems. * Assist with the preparation for external audit activities / inspections as requested. * Performs QA/QC duties in accordance with applicable ISO standards to ensure compliance, quality, and continuous improvement * Represent the Quality team where required at meetings, project reviews etc. *Reporting and Data Analysis:* * Ensure that all Quality related data is clearly documented and up to date. * Assist in the preparation of monthly reports on Quality metrics as required. * Provide RCA Report for customers where necessary. *Continuous Improvement:* * Identify and suggest improvements to current equipment, processes, and procedures to streamline the manufacturing activities while ensuring high standards of quality. * Be an active participant in Continuous Improvement Projects by delivering on actions, critiquing current methods and suggesting alternatives/improvements. * Deliver assigned actions from the company’s Improvement Log. *Customer Experience:* * Focus on the overall satisfaction and experience of customers. *General:* * Maintain awareness of industry trends, health \& safety requirements and the International (IEC), British (BS), European (EN) and other standards which apply to our business. * Contribute to a collaborative and innovative working environment. * Model and promote safe and tidy work environment to ensure that strict Health \& Safety and environmental guidelines are adhered to at all times. * Deliver duties in a professional manner, with confidentiality and discretion. * Complete all training as required for the role. * Support as needed if requested to carry out other duties within the business. **Required Qualifications and Skills** * 3rd level qualification in a technical or manufacturing discipline, preferably Electrical or Electronic. (Degree desirable but not essential) * Min 3 years’ experience working ideally in the switchgear industry, or alternatively working in Product Quality, Product Reliability, Customer Assurance. * Outstanding attention to detail with a keen eye for inconsistencies. * Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to challenge colleagues in a respectful manner. * A logical mindset with an inherent understanding of structured processes. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * Good Microsoft Office skills (Word; Excel; PowerPoint; Visio; Outlook; Teams) * Outstanding attention to detail. * Experience working with ISO quality standards. * Committed, resilient professional who shows integrity and drive in everything they do. * Full driving licence, fluency in English and eligible to work in Ireland. **Other details** * Full\-time, permanent position. * Based primarily on site in our office in Rathcoole, Co Dublin. * Travel to client sites may be required. * Occasional evening or weekend work to meet project deadlines or deal with client requirements. * Salary €45,000 – €55,000 depending on experience, with scope for progression. * Continuous training and further education support. * Employee Assistance Programme. * Bike to work scheme. * Regular social activities. *As an equal opportunities’ employer, we are committed to treating our employees equally in relation to age, civil status, disability, ethnicity and race, family status, gender, membership of the Traveller community, religion, and sexual orientation. We treat our employees equally in recruitment, pay, conditions, training, work experience and career progression.* Pay: €45,000\.00\-€55,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Application question(s): * What salary range are you targeting for your next role? title: Quality Assurance Engineer
description: **Tech that takes you places** We’re CarTrawler, the global travel tech company behind seamless connections between people, places and possibilities. From powering car rental to creating smarter ways to move, we make travel smoother for millions worldwide. Our culture is built on curiosity, collaboration and craic where every idea counts and every journey matters. Ready to make an impact? Let’s go places together. As a QA Engineer, you will help drive the delivery of high\-quality, reliable software across front\-end and back\-end systems. Working within a collaborative Agile team, you will design and execute manual test plans, identify defects and risks, and champion quality throughout the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC). You will work closely with engineers, product managers, designers, and operational teams to ensure quality is embedded from requirements through to release. This role requires strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and a proactive mindset focused on continuous improvement and delivering an excellent customer experience. **Your Journey starts here!** **What you’ll do as a QAEngineer** * Collaborate with Project Managers, Engineers, Product Managers, and Designers to ensure quality is embedded throughout the software development lifecycle. * Analyse business and technical requirements from multiple stakeholder perspectives, including end\-users, partners, operations, and customer support teams. * Design, develop, and execute detailed manual test cases, test scenarios, and regression test plans. * Support the development and maintenance of automated tests to improve software quality, regression coverage, and release confidence. * Identify, document, prioritise, and track defects through to resolution using tools such as Jira. * Support bug triage, escalation management, and root cause analysis activities. * Assess delivery risk and determine appropriate testing scope, coverage, and priorities. * Validate functional and non\-functional requirements across front\-end and back\-end systems. * Monitor and report on testing progress, quality metrics, risks, issues, and dependencies to relevant stakeholders. * Work closely with cross\-functional teams to ensure timely and high\-quality product releases. * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across QA processes, standards, methodologies, and tooling. * Support knowledge sharing and mentor junior QA team members where appropriate. * Advocate for best practices in software quality, usability, and customer experience. * Operate effectively within a fast\-paced, collaborative Agile environment. **What you’ll bring to the team** * 3\+ years of experience in Software Quality Assurance or a related testing role. * ISTQB Foundation Certification or equivalent software testing certification is desirable. * Experience working within Agile/Scrum or iterative software development environments. * Strong understanding of software testing principles, methodologies, and defect management processes. * Experience creating and executing manual test plans, test cases, and regression testing activities. * Experience developing, executing, and maintaining automated test suites using modern test automation frameworks and tools. * Familiarity with tools such as Jira, Confluence, Excel, or similar QA/project management tools. * Experience testing web applications, APIs, and back\-end services is advantageous. * Ability to analyse complex requirements and identify potential risks and edge cases. * Exposure to test automation frameworks or AI\-assisted testing tools is advantageous. * Understanding of modern QA practices including automation, CI/CD, and shift\-left testing principles is desirable * Strong organisational, prioritisation, and time management skills. * Ability to work independently while managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment. * Excellent analytical thinking and problem\-solving capabilities. * Strong attention to detail and commitment to delivering high\-quality outcomes. **Go on, give it a go!** We know that looking for a new job can feel a bit daunting but don’t let a touch of imposter syndrome hold you back. Even if you don’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you! Apply today to stay connected. At CarTrawler, we’re proud to be an equal opportunities employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for everyone. No matter your background, identity, or life experience, if you’re passionate about what you do, you’ll be welcomed here.

title: Regulatory Reporting and Controls
description: This position involves overseeing regulatory reporting and controls within a global Capital Markets and Corporate Lending bank in Dublin. The successful candidate will ensure data flow correct in the evolving regulatory rules landscape, overlap into corep and finrep/statistical returns. **Client Details** This opportunity is with a well\-established, globally renowned bank enjoying expansion. The company operates on a global scale and is recognised for its structured approach Capital Markets and Corporate Lending. **Description** **Regulatory Reporting and Controls** Equivalent levels/titles \- Senior AVP/Senior Associate/early VP Dublin City Centre \- 3 days per week in\-office hybrid working Banking \& Financial Services * Prepare and review regulatory reports to ensure accuracy and compliance with relevant standards \- sits across a mix of Corep, FinRep/Statistical Returns, and Data Flow/Controls. It is the last part (Controls) that this role will really focus on. * Monitor and enhance control frameworks to mitigate risks within the reporting process. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to address regulatory requirements and changes. * Analyse financial data to support regulatory submissions and business objectives. * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of regulatory guidelines and industry best practices. * Identify and implement process improvements for reporting efficiency. * Support audits and regulatory reviews by providing necessary documentation and insights. * Assist in the development of policies and procedures related to regulatory reporting and controls. **Profile** **Regulatory Reporting and Controls** Equivalent levels/titles \- Senior AVP/Senior Associate/early VP Dublin City Centre \- 3 days per week in\-office hybrid working Banking \& Financial Services A successful Regulatory Reporting and Controls professional should have: * A strong background in either Regulatory Reporting but with that Controls experience being key from a mid to large Bank; or could be a Manager to Senior Manager level Auditor of Banks (again given Control mindset that brings) from Big 4 to Top 60 Practice firms. * Proficiency in regulatory reporting processes and frameworks / or if from Practice then a genuine interest/well\-round around regulation. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Experience working with compliance and risk management systems. * A detail\-oriented mindset with the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively. * Strong communication skills to work collaboratively across teams. * Relevant professional qualifications in Accounting or Finance. **Job Offer** * A competitive salary ranging from €80,000 to €85,000 per annum. * Permanent position within a respected organisation in the Financial Services industry. * Opportunities for career progression and professional development. * Exposure to a global network and best practices in regulatory reporting. * Collaborative and supportive work environment in Dublin City Centre. If you are ready to take the next step in your career, apply today to join this exciting opportunity in Dublin. **Desired Skills and Experience** Regulatory Reporting and Controls within global Capital Markets and Corporate Lending bank in Dublin: ensure data flow correct in the evolving regulatory rules landscape, overlap into corep and finrep/statistical returns. From Practice Top 60\-Big 4 FS Bank Audit; or Regulatory Reporting with Controls in other Bank

title: Research Associate – UNSW Defence-funded project
description: This Job is based in Australia Research Associate – UNSW Defence\-funded project Apply now **Job no:** 539631 **Work type:** Full Time **Location:** Sydney, NSW **Categories:** Post Doctoral Research Associate **The Opportunity** The Research Associate will work on a Defence\-funded project to develop LLM\-agent\-driven tools that reconstruct network topology, services, and administrator intent from partial information during crises, enabling rapid situational awareness across diverse, multi\-vendor environments. This project is in collaboration with colleagues from the University of Sydney. Due to project requirements, only citizens of Australia, the Five Eyes Countries (New Zealand, Canada, the USA, and the UK), and NATO member countries will be considered. The role reports to Professor Salil Kanhere, Dr Rahat Masood and A/Prof. Gustavo Batista has no direct reports. * Salary, Level A \- $91,381 \- $121,838 per annum \+ 17% superannuation * Full Time * Fixed\-term contract: 9 months * Location: Kensington – Sydney, Australia **About UNSW** UNSW isn’t like other places you’ve worked. Yes, we’re a large organisation with a diverse and talented community; a community doing extraordinary things. But what makes us different isn’t only what we do, it’s how we do it. Together, we are driven to be thoughtful, practical, and purposeful in all we do. If you want a career where you can thrive, be challenged and do meaningful work, you’re in the right place. The School of Computer Science and Engineering is one of the largest and most prestigious computing schools in Australia. It offers undergraduate programs in Software Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, Cyber Security and Bioinformatics, as well as a number of combined degrees with other disciplines. It attracts excellent students with outstanding records in international competitions. At the postgraduate level, there is a large PhD research program and coursework programs at the Master’s and Graduate Diploma levels in Computing and Information Technology, and at the Graduate Certificate level in Computing. Our research mission is to be at the forefront of research into new and exciting innovations with profound national and international impact. For further information about the School, please visit http://www.cse.unsw.edu.au/ **Skills \& Experience** * PhD (or soon to be awarded) in computer science, electrical engineering or related area with demonstrated experience in computer networks and artificial intelligence. * Experience in research on computer networking, network protocols, routing protocols, network management and network performance measurement. * Hands\-on experience with networking tools and simulators. * Good understanding and experience with machine learning and Large Language Models (LLMs). * Demonstrated ability to conduct independent research with limited supervision. * Demonstrated track record of publications in top\-rated journals and conferences in the discipline. * Demonstrated ability to work in a team, collaborate across disciplines and build effective relationships. * Strong interpersonal skills with demonstrated ability to communicate and interact with a diverse range of stakeholders, external clients and students. * Ability to supervise undergraduate students or equivalent experience in the supervision of junior staff in related projects. **Pre\-Employment Checks** Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre\-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for. These pre\-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks: * National and International Criminal history checks * Entitlement to work and ID checks * Working With Children Checks * Completion of a Gender\-Based Violence Prevention Declaration * Verification of relevant qualifications * Verification of relevant professional membership * Employment history and reference checks * Financial responsibility assessments/checks * Medical Checks and Assessments Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW. To be considered you will hold Australian Working Rights or Australian Citizenship. Visa sponsorship is not available for this appointment. Additional details about the specific responsibilities for these positions can be found in the position description. This is available via JOBS@UNSW. **To Apply** Please click the apply now button and submit your CV, Cover Letter and Responses to the Skills and Experience. You should systematically address the Skills and Experience listed within the position description in your application. *Please note: Visa sponsorship is not available for this position.* Please note applications will not be accepted if sent to the contact listed below. **Contact** : Allyssar Hamoud – Talent Acquisition Associate E: a.hamoud@unsw.edu.au **Applications close:** 11:55 pm (Sydney time) on Sunday 5th July 2026 UNSW is committed to evolving a culture that embraces equity and supports a diverse and inclusive community where everyone can participate fairly, in a safe and respectful environment. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and encourage applications from people of diverse gender, sexual orientation, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people with disability and those with caring and family responsibilities. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options for eligible staff. The University reserves the right not to proceed with any appointment. title: Retail Associate
description: TK Maxx At TJX Europe, every day brings new opportunities for growth, exploration, and achievement.
